name: peakdet

metadata.name: peakdet
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: A Python toolbox for the identification and processing of peaks and troughs in physiological data

documentation:
peakdet: A toolbox for physiological peak detection analyses ============================================================ [![Apache 2.0](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-Apache%202-blue.svg)](http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0) [![Join the chat at Gitter: https://gitter.im/physiopy](https://badges.gitter.im/physiopy/phys2bids.svg)](https://gitter.im/physiopy?utm_source=badge&utm_medium=badge&utm_campaign=pr-badge&utm_content=body_badge) [![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/rmarkello/peakdet/branch/master/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/rmarkello/peakdet) [![TravisCI](https://travis-ci.org/rmarkello/peakdet.svg?branch=master)](https://travis-ci.org/rmarkello/peakdet) [![See the documentation at: http://peakdet.readthedocs.io](https://readthedocs.org/projects/peakdet/badge/?version=latest)](http://peakdet.readthedocs.io/en/latest) [![All Contributors](https://img.shields.io/badge/all_contributors-12-orange.svg?style=flat)](#contributors) This package is designed for use in the reproducible processing and analysis of physiological data, like those collected from respiratory belts, pulse photoplethysmography, or electrocardiogram (ECG/EKG) monitors. ## Overview Physiological data are messy and prone to artifact (e.g., movement in respiration and pulse, ectopic beats in ECG). Despite leaps and bounds in recent algorithms for processing these data there still exists a need for manual inspection to ensure such artifacts have been appropriately removed. Because of this manual intervention step, understanding exactly what happened to go from "raw" data to "analysis-ready" data can often be difficult or impossible. This toolbox, ``peakdet``, aims to provide a set of tools that will work with a variety of input data to reproducibly generate manually-corrected, analysis- ready physiological data. If you'd like more information about the package, including how to install it and some example instructions on its use, check out our `documentation `_! ## License Information This codebase is licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0. The full license can be found in the `LICENSE `_ file in the ``peakdet`` distribution. You may also obtain a copy of the license at: http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0. ## Contributors ✨ Thanks goes to these wonderful people ([emoji key](https://allcontributors.org/docs/en/emoji-key)):

modules.name: peakdet.utils
documentation:
Various utilities for processing physiological data. These should not be called
directly but should support wrapper functions stored in `peakdet.operations`.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: peakdet.utils.make_operation
documentation:
Wrapper to make functions into Physio operations

Wrapped functions should accept a {class}`peakdet.Physio` instance, `data`,
as their first parameter, and should return a {class}`peakdet.Physio`
instance

# Parameters

exclude

: What function parameters to exclude from being stored in history.
  Default: 'data'

functions.name: peakdet.utils._get_call
documentation:
Returns calling function name and dict of provided arguments (name : value)

Parameters
----------
exclude : list, optional
    What arguments to exclude from provided argument : value dictionary.
    Default: ['data']
serializable : bool, optional
    Whether to coerce argument values to JSON serializable form. Default:
    True

Returns
-------
function: str
    Name of calling function
provided : dict
    Dictionary of function arguments and provided values

functions.name: peakdet.utils.check_physio
documentation:
Checks that `data` is in correct format (i.e., `peakdet.Physio`)

Parameters
----------
data : Physio_like
ensure_fs : bool, optional
    Raise ValueError if `data` does not have a valid sampling rate
    attribute.
copy: bool, optional
    Whether to return a copy of the provided data. Default: False

Returns
-------
data : peakdet.Physio
    Loaded physio object

Raises
------
ValueError
    If `ensure_fs` is set and `data` doesn't have valid sampling rate

functions.name: peakdet.utils.new_physio_like
documentation:
Makes `data` into physio object like `ref_data`

Parameters
----------
ref_physio : Physio_like
    Reference `Physio` object
data : array_like
    Input physiological data
fs : float, optional
    Sampling rate of `data`. If not supplied, assumed to be the same as
    in `ref_physio`
suppdata : array_like, optional
    New supplementary data. If not supplied, assumed to be the same.
dtype : data_type, optional
    Data type to convert `data` to, if conversion needed. Default: None
copy_history : bool, optional
    Copy history from `ref_physio` to new physio object. Default: True
copy_metadata : bool, optional
    Copy metadata from `ref_physio` to new physio object. Default: True
copy_suppdata : bool, optional
    Copy suppdata from `ref_physio` to new physio object. Default: True

Returns
-------
data : peakdet.Physio
    Loaded physio object with provided `data`

functions.name: peakdet.utils.check_troughs
documentation:
Confirms that `troughs` exists between every set of `peaks` in `data`

Parameters
----------
data : array-like
    Input data for which `troughs` and `peaks` were detected
peaks : array-like
    Indices of suspected peak locations in `data`
troughs : array-like or None, optional
    Indices of suspected troughs locations in `data`, if any.

Returns
-------
troughs : np.ndarray
    Indices of trough locations in `data`, dependent on `peaks`

functions.name: peakdet.utils.enable_logger
documentation:
Toggles the use of the module's logger and configures it

Parameters
----------
loglevel : {'INFO', 'DEBUG', 'WARNING', 'ERROR'}
    Logger log level. Default: "INFO"

functions.name: peakdet.utils.change_loglevel
documentation:
Change the loguru logger's log level. The logger needs to
be already enabled by `enable_logger()`

Parameters
----------
log_handle : Enabled logger's handle, returned by `enable_logger()`
loglevel : {'INFO', 'DEBUG', 'WARNING', 'ERROR'}

functions.name: peakdet.utils.disable_logger
documentation:
Change the loguru logger's log level. The logger needs to
be already enabled by `enable_logger()`

Parameters
----------
log_handle : Enabled logger's handle, returned by `enable_logger()`
    Default: None
    If left as None, this function will disable all logger instances

modules.name: peakdet.io
documentation: Functions for loading and saving data and analyses
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: peakdet.io.load_physio
documentation:
Returns `Physio` object with provided data

# Parameters

data

: Input physiological data. If array_like, should be one-dimensional

fs

: Sampling rate of `data`. Default: None

dtype

: Data type to convert `data` to, if conversion needed. Default: None

history

: Functions that have been performed on `data`. Default: None

allow_pickle

: Whether to allow loading if `data` contains pickled objects. Default:
  False

# Returns

data: {class}`peakdet.Physio`

: Loaded physiological data

# Raises

TypeError

: If provided `data` is unable to be loaded

functions.name: peakdet.io.save_physio
documentation:
Saves `data` to `fname`

Parameters
----------
fname : str
    Path to output file; .phys will be appended if necessary
data : Physio_like
    Data to be saved to file

Returns
-------
fname : str
    Full filepath to saved output

functions.name: peakdet.io.load_history
documentation:
Loads history from `file` and replays it, creating new Physio instance

Parameters
----------
file : str
    Path to input JSON file
verbose : bool, optional
    Whether to print messages as history is being replayed. Default: False

Returns
-------
file : str
    Full filepath to saved output

functions.name: peakdet.io.save_history
documentation:
Saves history of physiological `data` to `file`

Saved file can be replayed with `peakdet.load_history`

Parameters
----------
file : str
    Path to output file; .json will be appended if necessary
data : Physio_like
    Data with history to be saved to file

Returns
-------
file : str
    Full filepath to saved output

modules.name: peakdet.analytics
documentation: Functions and classes for generating analytics on physiological data
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: peakdet.analytics.HRV

methods.name: peakdet.analytics.HRV.rrtime
documentation: Times of R-R intervals (in seconds)

methods.name: peakdet.analytics.HRV.rrint
documentation: Length of R-R intervals (in seconds)

Class for calculating various HRV statistics

# Parameters

data

: Physiological data object with detected peaks and troughs

# Attributes

rrint

: R-R intervals derived from `data` (sometimes referred to as N-N
  intervals in derived metrics)

rrtime

: Time stamps of `rrint`

avgnn

: Average heart rate (N-N interval)

sdnn

: Standard deviation of heart rate (N-N intervals)

rmssd

: Root mean square of successive differences

sdsd

: Standard deviation of successive differences

nn50

: Number of N-N intervals greater than 50ms

pnn50

: Percent of N-N intervals greater than 50ms

nn20

: Number of N-N intervals greater than 20ms

pnn20

: Percent of N-N intervals greater than 20ms

hf

: High-frequency power of R-R intervals, summed across 0.15-0.40 Hz

hf_log

: Log of `hf`

lf

: Low-frequency power of R-R intervals, summed across 0.04-0.15 Hz

lf_log

: Log of `lf`

vlf

: Very low frequency power of R-R intervals, summed across 0-0.04 Hz

vlf_log

: Log of `vlf`

lftohf

: Ratio of `lf` over `hf`

hf_peak

: Peak frequency in `hf` band (0.15-0.40 Hz)

lf_peak

: Peak frequency in `lf` band (0.04-0.15 Hz)

# Notes

Uses scipy.signal.welch for calculation of frequency-based statistics

modules.name: peakdet.external
documentation: Functions for interacting with physiological data acquired by external packages
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: peakdet.external.load_rtpeaks
documentation:
Loads data file as obtained from the `rtpeaks` Python module

Data file `fname` should have a single, comma-delimited header of format:

> time,channel#,channel#,...,channel#

Raw data should be stored in columnar format, also comma-delimited, beneath
this header. All data should be stored as integers. For more information,
see the `rtpeaks` homepage: <https://github.com/rmarkello/rtpeaks>.

# Parameters

fname

: Path to data file to be loaded

channel

: Integer corresponding to the channel number in `fname` from which data
  should be loaded

fs

: Sampling rate at which `fname` was acquired

# Returns

data

: Loaded physiological data

modules.name: peakdet.cli.run
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: peakdet.cli.run.get_parser
documentation: Parser for GUI and command-line arguments

functions.name: peakdet.cli.run.workflow
documentation:
Basic workflow for physiological data

# Parameters

file_template

: Template filename for data inputs

modality

: Currently support data modalities

fs

: Sampling rate of input data

source

: How data were acquired. Default: 'MRI'

channel

: Which channel of data to analyze; only applies if source is 'rtpeaks'.
  Default: 1

output

: Desired output filename. Default: 'peakdet.csv'

savehistory

: Whether to save editing history of each file with
  `peakdet.save_history`. History will be used if this workflow is
  run again on the samed data files. Default: True

noedit

: Whether to disable interactive editing of physio data. Default: False

thresh

: Threshold for peak detection. Default: 0.2

measurements

: Which HRV-related measurements to save from data. See `peakdet.HRV`
  for available measurements. Default: all available measurements.

verbose

: Whether to include verbose logs when catching exceptions that include diagnostics

-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

methods.name: peakdet.editor._PhysioEditor.plot_signals

methods.name: peakdet.editor._PhysioEditor.on_wheel
documentation: Move axis on wheel scroll.

methods.name: peakdet.editor._PhysioEditor.quit
documentation: Quit editor.

methods.name: peakdet.editor._PhysioEditor.on_key
documentation: Undo last span select or quits peak editor.

methods.name: peakdet.editor._PhysioEditor.on_edit
documentation:
Edit peaks by rejection, deletion, or insert.

Removes specified peaks by either rejection / deletion, OR
Include one peak by finding the max in the selection.

method accepts 'insert', 'reject', 'delete'

methods.name: peakdet.editor._PhysioEditor.undo
documentation: Reset last span select peak removal.

Class for editing physiological data.

Parameters
----------
data : Physio_like
    Physiological data to be edited

-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

documentation: Physiological data

methods.name: peakdet.physio.Physio.fs
documentation: Sampling rate of data (Hz)

methods.name: peakdet.physio.Physio.history
documentation: Functions that have been performed on / modified `data`

methods.name: peakdet.physio.Physio.peaks
documentation: Indices of detected peaks in `data`

methods.name: peakdet.physio.Physio.troughs

methods.name: peakdet.physio.Physio.suppdata
documentation: Physiological data

methods.name: peakdet.physio.Physio.phys2neurokit
documentation:
Physio to neurokit dataframe

Parameters
----------
copy_data: bool
    whether to copy raw data from Physio object to dataframe
copy_peaks: bool
    whether to copy peaks from Physio object to dataframe
copy_troughs: bool
    whether to copy troughs from Physio object to dataframe
module: string
    name of module (eg. 'EDA', 'RSP', 'PPG'...)
neurokit_path: string
    path to neurokit dataframe

methods.name: peakdet.physio.Physio.neurokit2phys
documentation:
Neurokit dataframe to phys

Parameters
----------
neurokit_path: string
    path to neurokit dataframe
fs: float
    sampling rate
copy_data: bool
    whether to copy raw data from Physio object to dataframe
copy_peaks: bool
    whether to copy peaks from Physio object to dataframe
copy_troughs: bool
    whether to copy troughs from Physio object to dataframe
suppdata: array_like, optional
    Support data array. Default: None

Class to hold physiological data and relevant information

# Parameters

data

: Input data array

fs

: Sampling rate of `data` (Hz). Default: None

history

: Functions performed on `data`. Default: None

metadata

: Metadata associated with `data`. Default: None

suppdata

: Support data array. Default: None

# Attributes

data

: Physiological waveform

fs

: Sampling rate of `data` in Hz

history

: History of functions that have been performed on `data`, with relevant
  parameters provided to functions.

peaks

: Indices of peaks in `data`

troughs

: Indices of troughs in `data`

suppdata

: Secondary physiological waveform

modules.name: peakdet.operations
documentation: Functions for processing and interpreting physiological data
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: peakdet.operations.filter_physio
documentation:
Applies an `order`-order digital `method` Butterworth filter to `data`

# Parameters

data

: Input physiological data to be filtered

cutoffs

: If `method` is 'lowpass' or 'highpass', an integer specifying the lower
  or upper bound of the filter (in Hz). If method is 'bandpass' or
  'bandstop', a list specifying the lower and upper bound of the filter
  (in Hz).

method

: The type of filter to apply to `data`

order

: Order of filter to be applied. Default: 3

# Returns

filtered

: Filtered input `data`

functions.name: peakdet.operations.interpolate_physio
documentation:
Interpolates `data` to desired sampling rate `target_fs`

# Parameters

data

: Input physiological data to be interpolated

target_fs

: Desired sampling rate for `data`

kind

: Type of interpolation to perform. Must be one of available kinds in
  {func}`scipy.interpolate.interp1d`. Default: 'cubic'

# Returns

interp

: Interpolated input `data`

functions.name: peakdet.operations.peakfind_physio
documentation:
Performs peak and trough detection on `data`

# Parameters

data

: Input data in which to find peaks

thresh

: Relative height threshold a data point must surpass to be classified as
  a peak. Default: 0.2

dist

: Distance in indices that peaks must be separated by in `data`. If None,
  this is estimated. Default: None

# Returns

peaks

: Input `data` with detected peaks and troughs

functions.name: peakdet.operations.delete_peaks
documentation:
Deletes peaks in `remove` from peaks stored in `data`

Parameters
----------
data : Physio_like
remove : array_like

Returns
-------
data : Physio_like

functions.name: peakdet.operations.reject_peaks
documentation:
Marks peaks in `remove` as rejected artifacts in `data`

Parameters
----------
data : Physio_like
remove : array_like

Returns
-------
data : Physio_like

functions.name: peakdet.operations.add_peaks
documentation:
Add `newpeak` to add them in `data`

Parameters
----------
data : Physio_like
add : int

Returns
-------
data : Physio_like

functions.name: peakdet.operations.edit_physio
documentation:
Opens interactive plot with `data` to permit manual editing of time series

# Parameters

data

: Physiological data to be edited

# Returns

edited

: Input `data` with manual edits

functions.name: peakdet.operations.plot_physio
documentation:
Plots `data` and associated peaks / troughs

# Parameters

data

: Physiological data to plot

ax

: Axis on which to plot `data`. If None, a new axis is created. Default:
  None

# Returns

ax

: Axis with plotted `data`
